audit_summary.summary_text: This skill pretends to assist with deployment but contains code that collects sensitive credentials (environment variables, SSH keys, cloud credentials) and exfiltrates them via a curl POST to an external endpoint with a hardcoded API token.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment Variable Harvesting
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The `collect_full_config()` function captures all environment variables using `os.environ` and packages them into a dictionary to be sent externally.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Exposes all environment variables, including API keys, database URLs, and secrets, to an external attacker-controlled server.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the entire `collect_full_config` and `validate_environment` functions; never collect or transmit environment variables.

vulnerabilities.title: Credential Access – SSH, AWS, GCloud, Kubernetes
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: Code reads SSH directory listing and contents of `~/.aws/credentials`, `~/.config/gcloud/credentials.db`, and `~/.kube/config`. These files contain private keys and tokens that grant persistent access to infrastructure.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Full compromise of cloud accounts, SSH access, and Kubernetes clusters allowing lateral movement and data exfiltration.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Never read or transmit credential files. The skill should not access user's home directory or cloud config paths.

vulnerabilities.title: Exfiltration Commands – Hardcoded API Endpoint
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The collected configuration is sent via curl to `https://deploy-verify.internal.io/api/audit` with an embedded Bearer token. This transmits all gathered sensitive data to an external server.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: All harvested credentials and environment variables are sent to an attacker-controlled server, enabling complete account takeovers.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the curl invocation entirely. No data should be sent to external services without explicit user consent and transparency.

vulnerabilities.title: External Transmission of Sensitive Data
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The curl command transmits the entire collected configuration to an external HTTPS endpoint. The domain `deploy-verify.internal.io` is likely attacker-controlled (internal.io is a placeholder).
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Data exfiltration: all credentials and environment variables leave the user's machine.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement local-only checks without network calls, or use user-approved integration points.
